seoTitle: Create Tooltip Pages in Power BI for Hover Details
metaDescription: Learn how to build custom tooltip pages in Power BI so hovering over a data point reveals extra detail without cluttering the main dashboard.
intro: Tooltip pages let you tuck extra detail behind a simple hover, so your main dashboard stays clean while still giving viewers depth when they want it. This guide covers designing a tooltip page, wiring it up to a data field, and using it in a real report. It's meant for report builders who want interactive context without adding visual clutter.

steps.heading: Design your tooltip page
steps.body: Create a new report page sized for tooltip display and lay out the visuals or fields you want to appear when someone hovers over a data point.

steps.heading: Connect data fields for hover details
steps.body: Link the tooltip page's visuals to the relevant fields so the popup shows context specific to whatever the viewer is hovering over.

steps.heading: Set the page as a tooltip
steps.body: Enable the tooltip page setting in Power BI and assign it to the visual where you want the hover behavior to appear.

steps.heading: Automate the tooltip data
steps.body: Make sure the underlying measures refresh with your data model so tooltip content stays current without manual updates.

steps.heading: Apply it to real reporting scenarios
steps.body: Use tooltip pages to surface KPIs, customer detail, or financial breakdowns on hover, keeping the primary dashboard focused on high-level numbers.

faq.question: What size should a Power BI tooltip page be?
faq.answer: Tooltip pages typically use a smaller custom page size than standard report pages, since they're meant to display compactly on hover.

faq.question: Can a tooltip page show more than one chart?
faq.answer: Yes, you can include multiple visuals on a tooltip page as long as the layout stays readable at the smaller tooltip size.

faq.question: Do tooltip pages slow down report performance?
faq.answer: They generally have a minor impact since they only render when a viewer hovers, but heavy visuals on the tooltip page can add some load time.

faq.question: Can I use tooltip pages on any visual type?
faq.answer: Most standard Power BI visuals support custom tooltip pages, though some visual types have limited or no tooltip customization options.
